instructions.instruction: Use Window TVFs (TUMBLE/HOP/SESSION/CUMULATE) for windowed aggregations, not legacy GROUP BY TUMBLE() syntax
instructions.original_snippets: Windowed aggregation | Window TVFs (TUMBLE/HOP/SESSION/CUMULATE) ... FROM TABLE(TUMBLE(TABLE orders, DESCRIPTOR(event_time), INTERVAL '1' HOUR))
instructions.relevant_when: when writing windowed aggregation queries

instructions.instruction: Always declare watermarks for event-time processing using WATERMARK FOR col AS expression
instructions.original_snippets: Always declare watermarks for event-time processing ... WATERMARK FOR event_time AS event_time - INTERVAL '5' SECOND
instructions.relevant_when: when creating tables with event-time columns

instructions.instruction: Use bounded out-of-orderness watermark strategy (event_time - INTERVAL 'n' SECOND) as default
instructions.original_snippets: event_time - INTERVAL 'n' SECOND — bounded out-of-orderness ... event_time — strictly ascending (no late data) ... SOURCE_WATERMARK() — preserve source watermarks
instructions.relevant_when: when choosing a watermark strategy

instructions.instruction: Use interval joins for time-bounded stream-to-stream joins with BETWEEN syntax
instructions.original_snippets: Interval join (time-bounded) ... WHERE o.order_id = s.order_id AND s.ship_time BETWEEN o.order_time AND o.order_time + INTERVAL '4' HOUR
instructions.relevant_when: when joining two streams with time constraints

instructions.instruction: Use temporal joins with FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF for point-in-time lookups against versioned tables
instructions.original_snippets: Temporal join (point-in-time lookup) ... JOIN currency_rates FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF o.order_time AS r ON o.currency = r.currency
instructions.relevant_when: when joining with a versioned dimension table using event time

instructions.instruction: Use lookup joins with FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF proc_time for external dimension tables
instructions.original_snippets: Lookup join (external table) ... JOIN customers FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF o.proc_time AS c ON o.customer_id = c.id
instructions.relevant_when: when enriching a stream with data from an external database

instructions.instruction: Use ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY key ORDER BY time DESC) WHERE rn = 1 for deduplication
instructions.original_snippets: Deduplication ... ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY event_time DESC) AS rn ... WHERE rn = 1
instructions.relevant_when: when deduplicating a stream to keep latest or first record per key

instructions.instruction: Use MATCH_RECOGNIZE for complex event processing and pattern detection
instructions.original_snippets: Pattern detection | MATCH_RECOGNIZE ... MATCH_RECOGNIZE (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY event_time MEASURES ... PATTERN (A+ B) DEFINE ...)
instructions.relevant_when: when detecting patterns in event streams

instructions.instruction: Configure state TTL to prevent unbounded state growth — unbounded state = memory explosion
instructions.original_snippets: State TTL matters — unbounded state = memory explosion ... SET 'table.exec.state.ttl' = '24h'
instructions.relevant_when: when using stateful operations like joins or aggregations without windows

instructions.instruction: Know your changelog mode — insert-only vs updating affects downstream consumers
instructions.original_snippets: Know your changelog mode — insert-only vs updating affects downstream ... Insert-only: Append-only streams (logs, events) ... Upsert: Updates keyed by primary key
instructions.relevant_when: when deciding sink connector type and downstream compatibility

instructions.instruction: Use upsert-kafka connector with PRIMARY KEY for tables that need UPDATE/DELETE semantics
instructions.original_snippets: Upsert to Kafka ... 'connector' = 'upsert-kafka' ... PRIMARY KEY (customer_id) NOT ENFORCED
instructions.relevant_when: when writing aggregation results or CDC data to Kafka

instructions.instruction: Java UDFs must extend ScalarFunction with a public eval() method; handle null inputs
instructions.original_snippets: public class MyUpperCase extends ScalarFunction { public String eval(String s) { return s == null ? null : s.toUpperCase(); } }
instructions.relevant_when: when creating a Java scalar UDF

instructions.instruction: Java Table functions must extend TableFunction and use collect() to emit rows; use @FunctionHint for output type
instructions.original_snippets: @FunctionHint(output = @DataTypeHint("ROW<word STRING, length INT>")) public class SplitFunction extends TableFunction<Row> { public void eval(String str) { ... collect(Row.of(s, s.length())); } }
instructions.relevant_when: when creating a Java table function (UDTF)

instructions.instruction: Use maven-shade-plugin to build fat JARs for UDF deployment; mark flink-table-api-java as provided scope
instructions.original_snippets: maven-shade-plugin ... <scope>provided</scope> ... flink-table-api-java
instructions.relevant_when: when building UDF JAR for deployment

instructions.instruction: Deploy UDFs to Confluent Cloud via: build JAR, upload artifact with confluent flink artifact create, register with CREATE FUNCTION ... USING JAR 'confluent-artifact://cfa-xxxxx'
instructions.original_snippets: confluent flink artifact create my-udf --cloud aws --region us-east-1 --artifact-file target/my-udf-1.0.jar ... CREATE FUNCTION my_upper AS 'com.example.MyUpperCase' USING JAR 'confluent-artifact://cfa-xxxxx'
instructions.relevant_when: when deploying a UDF to Confluent Cloud

instructions.instruction: Confluent Cloud does not support aggregate UDFs, temporary functions, ALTER FUNCTION, UDFs with MATCH_RECOGNIZE, or vararg functions
instructions.original_snippets: Confluent Cloud limits: ... No aggregate UDFs (only scalar and table functions) ... No MATCH_RECOGNIZE with UDFs
instructions.relevant_when: when choosing UDF type for Confluent Cloud deployment

instructions.instruction: Confluent Cloud max limits: 10 UDFs per statement, 100 artifacts per environment, JDK 17 max, 100 MB max artifact size
instructions.original_snippets: Max 10 UDFs per statement ... Max 100 artifacts per environment ... JDK 17 max for uploaded JARs
instructions.relevant_when: when planning UDF deployment to Confluent Cloud

instructions.instruction: Use ConfluentSettings.fromGlobalVariables() to create TableEnvironment for Confluent Cloud Java Table API
instructions.original_snippets: TableEnvironment env = TableEnvironment.create(ConfluentSettings.fromGlobalVariables())
instructions.relevant_when: when writing Java Table API code for Confluent Cloud

instructions.instruction: Use ConfluentTools.printMaterializedLimit() and collectMaterializedLimit() for displaying results in Confluent Cloud
instructions.original_snippets: ConfluentTools.printMaterializedLimit(table, 100) ... List<Row> rows = ConfluentTools.collectMaterializedLimit(table, 100)
instructions.relevant_when: when collecting or printing results from Confluent Cloud Flink

instructions.instruction: Confluent Cloud tables have system columns $rowtime (event time from Kafka timestamp) and $headers (Kafka headers)
instructions.original_snippets: $rowtime | TIMESTAMP_LTZ(3) | Event time from Kafka timestamp ... $headers | MAP<STRING, BYTES> | Kafka headers
instructions.relevant_when: when querying Confluent Cloud managed tables

instructions.instruction: Use fully qualified table names in Confluent Cloud: `cluster`.`database`.`topic_name`
instructions.original_snippets: SELECT * FROM `cluster`.`database`.`topic_name` ... Catalog Structure: <environment> > <kafka-cluster> > <database> > topic
instructions.relevant_when: when referencing tables in Confluent Cloud Flink SQL

instructions.instruction: Process Table Functions (PTFs) extend ProcessTableFunction and support full state access, timers, and changelog handling
instructions.original_snippets: PTFs are the most powerful function kind in Flink — user-defined operators with full access to state, timers, and changelogs ... extends ProcessTableFunction<Row>
instructions.relevant_when: when implementing stateful stream processing logic beyond what SQL or simple UDFs can express

instructions.instruction: PTF set semantics use @ArgumentHint(SET_SEMANTIC_TABLE) with PARTITION BY for stateful per-key processing; row semantics use @ArgumentHint(ROW_SEMANTIC_TABLE) for stateless per-row processing
instructions.original_snippets: Set Semantics (Stateful, Partitioned) ... @ArgumentHint(SET_SEMANTIC_TABLE) Row input ... Row Semantics (Stateless, Per-Row) ... @ArgumentHint(ROW_SEMANTIC_TABLE) Row input
instructions.relevant_when: when choosing between stateful and stateless PTF processing

instructions.instruction: Use @StateHint with ttl annotation for PTF state to prevent unbounded state growth
instructions.original_snippets: @StateHint(ttl = "30d") ... @StateHint(ttl = "24h") ... @StateHint(ttl = "7d")
instructions.relevant_when: when managing state in Process Table Functions

instructions.instruction: Always provide uid parameter for production PTFs for savepoint compatibility
instructions.original_snippets: Always provide uid for production PTFs — required for savepoint compatibility ... uid => 'my-ptf-v1'
instructions.relevant_when: when deploying PTFs to production

instructions.instruction: Use @OnTimer annotation for timer callbacks in PTFs; register timers with ctx.registerOnTime()
instructions.original_snippets: @OnTimer public void onTimer(Context ctx, ...) ... ctx.registerOnTime(eventTime + sessionTimeoutMillis)
instructions.relevant_when: when implementing time-based logic in PTFs (session detection, delayed processing)

instructions.instruction: Use avro-confluent format with Schema Registry options for Avro serialization with Kafka connector
instructions.original_snippets: 'format' = 'avro-confluent' ... 'avro-confluent.url' = 'https://SR_ENDPOINT' ... 'avro-confluent.basic-auth.credentials-source' = 'USER_INFO'
instructions.relevant_when: when reading/writing Avro data with Schema Registry

instructions.instruction: Use debezium-json format for consuming CDC data from Debezium connectors
instructions.original_snippets: 'format' = 'debezium-json' ... CREATE TABLE cdc_customers (...) WITH ('connector' = 'kafka', 'topic' = 'dbserver1.inventory.customers', 'format' = 'debezium-json')
instructions.relevant_when: when consuming Change Data Capture events

instructions.instruction: Use Statement Sets (BEGIN STATEMENT SET; ... END;) to insert into multiple sinks from the same source
instructions.original_snippets: Statement Sets (Multi-Insert) ... BEGIN STATEMENT SET; INSERT INTO high_value_orders SELECT ...; INSERT INTO regular_orders SELECT ...; END;
instructions.relevant_when: when routing events to multiple output tables from one source

instructions.instruction: Use EXPLAIN to validate SQL query plans without executing
instructions.original_snippets: EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM orders WHERE amount > 100 ... Use EXPLAIN to validate without executing
instructions.relevant_when: when debugging or validating Flink SQL queries

instructions.instruction: Confluent Cloud does not support DataStream API, batch mode, custom connectors, or CREATE DATABASE/CATALOG
instructions.original_snippets: DataStream API | Not supported (Table API only) ... Batch mode | Not supported (streaming only) ... CREATE DATABASE | Not supported ... CREATE CATALOG | Not supported
instructions.relevant_when: when planning what features to use on Confluent Cloud

instructions.instruction: Use savepoints for job updates: stop with savepoint, delete old statement, create new, resume from savepoint
instructions.original_snippets: confluent flink savepoint create --statement my-job ... confluent flink statement delete my-job ... confluent flink statement resume my-job-v2 --savepoint sp-xxxxx
instructions.relevant_when: when upgrading or updating a running Flink job

instructions.instruction: Use CAST(ms AS BIGINT) instead of INTERVAL for PTF arguments due to FLINK-37618
instructions.original_snippets: Due to FLINK-37618, use CAST(5000 AS BIGINT) instead of INTERVAL '5' SECONDS ... INTERVAL arguments not supported (FLINK-37618)
instructions.relevant_when: when passing time parameters to Process Table Functions

instructions.instruction: Regular joins without time bounds result in unbounded state growth — prefer interval, temporal, or lookup joins
instructions.original_snippets: CAUTION: State grows unboundedly ... Regular Join (Unbounded State Warning!) ... Use interval join ... Or temporal join ... Or configure TTL
instructions.relevant_when: when joining two streams

instructions.instruction: Lookup joins require processing time attribute (PROCTIME()), not event time
instructions.original_snippets: Lookup join requires processing time attribute ... Add processing time column ... proc_time AS PROCTIME()
instructions.relevant_when: when implementing lookup joins against external tables

instructions.instruction: Use Python @udf decorator with result_type for scalar UDFs; use func_type='pandas' for vectorized performance
instructions.original_snippets: @udf(result_type=DataTypes.STRING()) def to_upper(s: str) -> str: ... @udf(result_type=DataTypes.STRING(), func_type='pandas') def to_upper_vectorized(s):
instructions.relevant_when: when creating Python UDFs for Flink
